answer: The ghazal's opening couplet states that a sigh must wait an entire age (a lifetime) before it takes effect. The line 'A sigh must wait an age to take its flight' conveys that the emotional impact of a sigh — its reaching the beloved — takes so long that one would not even survive to see it happen. This is underscored by the second half of the couplet: 'Who lives to conquer your dark hair's long night?' — implying the wait is effectively as long as a lifetime, and possibly longer than any single life. The ghazal thus frames the sigh as an expression of longing so deep that its effect is deferred beyond the lover's own existence.
